# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for kong plugin misconfiguration and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

_format_version: "2.1"

plugins:
  - name: cors
    config:
      origins:
        - "https://app1.example.com"
        - "https://app2.example.com"
        - "https://*.example.com"
      methods:
        - "GET"
        - "POST"
        - "PUT"
        - "DELETE"
        - "OPTIONS"
      headers:
        - "Content-Type"
        - "Authorization"
        - "X-Requested-With"
        - "Accept"
        - "Origin"
      exposed_headers:
        - "Access-Control-Allow-Origin"
        - "Access-Control-Allow-Methods"
        - "Access-Control-Allow-Headers"
      max_age: 3600
      credentials: true
    enabled: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https

  - name: key-auth
    config:
      key_in_body: false
      key_in_header: true
      key_name: "X-API-Key"
      hide_credentials: false
      anonymous: ""
    enabled: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https

  - name: rate-limiting
    config:
      minute: 1000
      hour: 10000
      policy: "local"
      fault_tolerant: true
      hide_client_headers: false
      sync_rate: -1
      redis:
        host: "redis"
        port: 6379
        password: "securepassword"
        database: 0
        timeout: 2000
    enabled: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https

services:
  - name: example-service
    url: "http://backend-service:8080"
    routes:
      - name: example-route
        paths:
          - "/api/*"
        plugins:
          - name: cors
            config:
              origins:
                - "https://app1.example.com"
                - "https://app2.example.com"
              methods:
                - "GET"
                - "POST"
                - "PUT"
                - "DELETE"
              headers:
                - "Content-Type"
                - "Authorization"
              credentials: true
            enabled: true
          - name: key-auth
            config:
              key_in_header: true
              key_name: "X-API-Key"
            enabled: true
          - name: rate-limiting
            config:
              minute: 500
              hour: 5000
              policy: "local"
              fault_tolerant: true
            enabled: true

  - name: secure-service
    url: "http://secure-backend:8080"
    plugins:
      - name: cors
        config:
          origins:
            - "https://secure.example.com"
          methods:
            - "GET"
            - "POST"
          headers:
            - "Content-Type"
            - "Authorization"
          credentials: false
        enabled: true
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key_in_header: true
          key_name: "X-API-Key"
        enabled: true
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 100
          hour: 1000
          policy: "local"
          fault_tolerant: true
        enabled: true
